{{Expand Japanese|十二神社 (天理市)}}

{{Infobox religious building
| name = Juuni Shrine
| native_name = 十二神社
| mapframe = yes
| mapframe-zoom = 15
| mapframe-wikidata = yes
| mapframe-point = none

}}

{{nihongo|'''Juuni Shrine'''|十二神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

* {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}}
* {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}}

* {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
* {{ill|Q135160338|ltq=Q135160338|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}}

== Japanese content ==
{{出典の明記|date=June 20, 2015 (Sat) 04:19 (UTC)}}
{{神社
 | 名称=Jūni Shrine
 | 画像={{画像募集中|cat=Tenri}}
 | 所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|qid=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}} Takenouchi-chō 865
 | 位置={{Coord|34|34|49.4|N|135|51|10.7|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
 | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kuni no Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Kuni no Satsuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|qid=Q5966692|lt=Kuni no Satsuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uhijini-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Suhijini-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtomichi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtobe-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Omodaru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ayakashikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|qid=Q813858}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|qid=Q682306}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
 | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
 | 本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} cypress-bark roof
 | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|qid=Q2919|1=October 15}}
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Jūni Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}}
}}
'''Jūni Shrine''' (じゅうにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tenri|Q502196|qid=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}.

It is a candidate shrine for “Yatsugi Shrine” in Yamabe District, Yamato Province, listed as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (other candidate shrines are {{ill|lt=Yatsugi Shrine|Q11431328|qid=Q11431328|1=Yatsugi Shrine}} in Otsuki-chō and {{ill|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine (Tenri)|Q11431329|qid=Q11431329|lt=Yatsurugi Shrine|1=Mirura Yano}} in Taishō-chō).

=== Enshrined deities ===
Its deities are the 11 deities of the {{ill|lt=Seven Divine Generations|Q3275893|qid=Q3275893|1=Kamiyonanayo}} in the main text of the {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}, plus {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}; the name “Jūni Shrine” derives from these twelve deities. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called Jūnisha Daimyōjin.
*{{ill|qid=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Kami|Q478504|qid=Q478504|lt=Kuni no Tokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}
*{{ill|lt=Kuni no Satsuchi-no-Mikoto|Q5966692|qid=Q5966692|lt=Kuni no Satsuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}}
*{{ill|lt=Toyokumonu|Q4387158|qid=Q4387158|lt=Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto}}
*{{ill|lt=Uhijini and Suhijini|Q9090923|qid=Q9090923|lt=Uhijini-no-Mikoto and Suhijini-no-Mikoto|1=Uhijini and Suhijini}}
*{{ill|lt=Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|Q11073597|qid=Q11073597|lt=Ōtomichi-no-Mikoto and Ōtobe-no-Mikoto|1=Otonoji and Otonobe}}
*{{ill|lt=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|Q11152535|qid=Q11152535|lt=Omodaru-no-Mikoto and Ayakashikone-no-Mikoto|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone}}
*{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} and {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}
*{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}}

=== History ===
It is at the northeastern back of the Takenouchi settlement, a rare {{ill|lt=ring-moated settlement|Q60995739|qid=Q60995739|1=Moated settlements}} in the {{ill|lt=Nara Basin|Q10942269|qid=Q10942269|1=Nara Basin}} located above 100 m elevation. The moat of Takenouchi settlement still remains on the west side.

According to tradition, in the Edo period the precinct of {{ill|lt=Yatsugi Shrine|Q11431328|qid=Q11431328|1=Yatsugi Shrine}} (ubusunagami of Otsuki Village) was exchanged with Sagizuka Pond in Takenouchi Village, which is the current site of Jūni Shrine. The original ubusunagami of Takenouchi Village was Hakusan Gongen-sha enshrining {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|qid=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}}, but it was {{ill|lt=merged|Q11590700|qid=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} into this shrine in the Meiji period.

=== Precincts ===
There is a sacred stone in front of the worship hall, and a custom of laughing “wahaha,” then circling it three times before worship. Precinct shrines include Nanahashira Shrine and Taga Shrine.

North of this shrine is an area locals call “Hi no Tani.” “Yatsugi” is said to be a changed form of “Yatsurugi,” and because “Hi no Tani” appears often in origin tales of {{ill|lt=Yatsurugi Shrines|Q11390593|qid=Q11390593|1=Yatsurugi Shrine (disambiguation)}} in many regions, they are thought to be related.
